primary_accounting_issue: Déterminer, en IFRS, sur quelle base un investisseur détient le pouvoir sur une entité lorsqu’il existe plusieurs parties détenant des droits, une influence ou une participation sur cette entité

authority_resolution.selection_reason: I applied the precedence rule strictly: IFRS supersedes IAS, and IFRS 10 directly governs the assessment of control and power over an investee, whereas IFRS 11 and IAS 28 address distinct alternative outcomes only if individual control is not present.
authority_resolution.residual_uncertainty: Low uncertainty because the question is framed around which investor has power, and IFRS 10 is the direct governing standard for identifying control.

primary_authority.reason: This standard directly governs whether one investor has control through power, variable returns and the ability to use power to affect returns.

supporting_authority.reason: This standard provides the distinct alternative model of joint control when no single party controls the arrangement alone.
This standard provides the distinct alternative model of significant influence when an investor has influence but not control or joint control.

peripheral_authority.reason: This standard concerns related party disclosures and does not govern which investor has power for control assessment.

treatment_families.family: Individual control assessment based on power over relevant activities, variable returns, and linkage between power and returns
Collective control requiring unanimous consent over relevant activities
Ability to participate in financial and operating policy decisions without control or joint control

approaches.label: Control
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 10 identifies control as the primary model when one investor has power over relevant activities, exposure to variable returns, and the ability to use that power to affect returns.

approaches.label: Joint control
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 11 identifies joint control as the alternative model when the relevant activities require unanimous consent of the parties sharing control.

approaches.label: Significant influence
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 28 identifies significant influence as the alternative model when an investor can participate in policy decisions but does not have control or joint control.
